title: Rootless container: no internet connectivity (80, 443, while DNS worked) on v5.1.0-dev
body:
### Issue Description

Rootless containers are unable to connect to internet after upgrading from v4.4 to v5.1-dev

Version:
```bash
$ podman version
Client:       Podman Engine
Version:      5.1.0-dev-076902b45
API Version:  5.1.0-dev-076902b45
Go Version:   go1.20.12
Built:        Mon Apr 15 02:00:00 2024
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```

### Reproduce the issues + results you received

1. Pull and start the `libpod/banner` container
    ```$ podman run -dt --name webserver --net podman -p 8081:80 quay.io/libpod/banner```

2. Check that the container is up, running and working:
    ```
    $ wget localhost:8081 -q -O -
       ___          __              
      / _ \___  ___/ /_ _  ___ ____ 
     / ___/ _ \/ _  /  ' \/ _ `/ _ \
    /_/   \___/\_,_/_/_/_/\_,_/_//_/

    ```
    this works fine, the banner is shown

3. Check if the container has internet access
    ```
    $ podman exec --tty --interactive systemd-ingress wget --spider -T1 https://example.com
    Connecting to example.com (93.184.216.34:443)
    wget: download timed out
    ```

4. Check the host connectivity
    ```
    $ wget --spider -T1 https://example.com
    Spider mode enabled. Check if remote file exists.
    --2024-04-15 17:34:21--  https://example.com/
    Resolving example.com (example.com)... 93.184.216.34, 2606:2800:220:1:248:1893:25c8:1946
    Connecting to example.com (example.com)|93.184.216.34|:443... connected.
    HTTP request sent, awaiting response... 200 OK
    Length: 648 [text/html]
```

### Describe the results you expected

Internet connectivity (http/https) should work within the container (as was working with podman v4.4)

### podman info output

### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

Rootless

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

### Additional environment details

_No response_

### Additional information

Running the same with podman v4.4 worked without issues
Moving to the latest rolling-testing release (sorry. but I was not able to get Alamalinux to install the stable 5.1.0 tagged release :shrug: )

I discovered the issue while having a caddyserver container failing to obtain the certificates, which worked before, but it reported network timeouts with v5.1.0-dev release

comments.body:
passt-0^20230818.g0af928e-4.el9.x86_64

You passt version is outdated there have been many bug fixes since then. Please install the latest pasta (package passt) version and test again. check https://passt.top/passt/about/#availability

Thanks @Luap99 for the hint, updating `passt` worked.

On AlmaLinux  I run:
-
```
# dnf remove podman -y
# dnf copr enable sbrivio/passt -y
# dnf copr enable rhcontainerbot/podman-next -y
# dnf install passt -y
# dnf install podman -y
```

I got:
- podman version: `5.1.0-dev-2364b9073`
- passt version: `passt 0^20240405.g954589b-1`

And now my tests described above are green

Thanks to @sbrivio-rh for providing the copr's repository

ps: @sbrivio-rh your surname sounds familiar, looks like we might be from the same area :)

>     * passt version: `passt 0^20240405.g954589b-1`

I'm not sure how AlmaLinux sources package versions, but, if it helps, as I also maintain the CentOS Stream [package](https://gitlab.com/redhat/centos-stream/rpms/passt): the latest version available there is definitely more recent than the one you were using.

Not as recent as the one from my Copr, though, and it wasn't tested with Podman 5.0.

> Thanks to @sbrivio-rh for providing the copr's repository

You're welcome!

> ps: @sbrivio-rh your surname sounds familiar, looks like we might be from the same area :)

Quite likely, yes. :)

With AlmaLinux I am getting some weird behavior on the install, tho:

```
# dnf copr enable rhcontainerbot/podman-next -y
# dnf copr enable sbrivio/passt -y
# dnf install podman ← this installs the latest -dev release and passt vpasst-selinux-0^20240405.g954589b-1.el9.noarch
```
All good so far, podman works and containers are all OK.

Unfortunately the next `# dnf update` shows:

```
Error: 
 Problem 1: cannot install the best update candidate for package iptables-legacy-1.8.8-6.el9.2.x86_64
  - nothing provides (iptables-libs(x86-64) = 1.8.10-2.el9 or iptables-libs(x86-64) = 1.8.10-2.el9_1) needed by iptables-legacy-1.8.10-2.2.el9.next.x86_64 from epel-next
 Problem 2: cannot install the best update candidate for package passt-selinux-0^20240405.g954589b-1.el9.noarch
  - nothing provides passt = 0.git.2022_08_04.b516d15 needed by passt-selinux-0.git.2022_08_04.b516d15-0.el9.x86_64 from copr:copr.fedorainfracloud.org:sbrivio:passt
 Problem 3: problem with installed package iptables-legacy-1.8.8-6.el9.2.x86_64
  - package iptables-legacy-1.8.8-6.el9.2.x86_64 from @System requires iptables-legacy-libs(x86-64) = 1.8.8-6.el9.2, but none of the providers can be installed
  - package iptables-legacy-1.8.8-6.el9.2.x86_64 from epel requires iptables-legacy-libs(x86-64) = 1.8.8-6.el9.2, but none of the providers can be installed
  - cannot install both iptables-legacy-libs-1.8.10-2.2.el9.next.x86_64 from epel-next and iptables-legacy-libs-1.8.8-6.el9.2.x86_64 from @System
  - cannot install both iptables-legacy-libs-1.8.10-2.2.el9.next.x86_64 from epel-next and iptables-legacy-libs-1.8.8-6.el9.2.x86_64 from epel
  - cannot install the best update candidate for package iptables-legacy-libs-1.8.8-6.el9.2.x86_64
  - nothing provides (iptables-libs(x86-64) = 1.8.10-2.el9 or iptables-libs(x86-64) = 1.8.10-2.el9_1) needed by iptables-legacy-1.8.10-2.2.el9.next.x86_64 from epel-next
(try to add '--allowerasing' to command line to replace conflicting packages or '--skip-broken' to skip uninstallable packages or '--nobest' to use not only best candidate packages)
```

Will try to find a solution for that!

OT:
@sbrivio-rh fun fact, I used to live next to a town called Brivio too :grinning:
